item.title: Iran War Spurs States to Consider Gas Tax Halt as Fuel Costs Surge
item.description: The war in Iran is prompting states across the country to consider temporarily cutting fuel taxes, a relief measure that could cost them millions of dollars.

item.title: Argentina Tweaks FX Rules After Cost of Getting Dollars Out Surges
item.description: Argentina’s government issued new rules for investors moving money out of the country after increased use of the parallel foreign-exchange market drove the cost of such trades to its highest in a year.

item.title: Vista Credit Sets Sights on Hard-Hit Software Loans Via New Fund
item.description: Vista Equity Partners’ credit-investing arm is raising $250 million for a new fund that will buy the beaten-down debt of software companies, seeking to capitalize on recent selloffs tied to concerns over artificial intelligence.

item.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Files for IPO in Defense Tech Boom
item.description: Hawkeye 360 Inc. filed for an initial public offering, joining a string of defense technology firms seeking to go public this year amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Switzerland Aims to Finalize US Tariff Accord by the End of July
item.description: Switzerland aims to finalize a US trade deal by the end of July, with the European nation pushing for assurances it won’t face higher duties than rival countries.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Drops to Record Low on Price Concerns
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in recent weeks to a record low, indicating Americans’ increasing worries about mounting inflation due to the Iran war.

item.title: Iceland to Cut Petrol Tax as Costlier Oil Risks Price Outlook
item.description: Iceland will temporarily lower taxes on gasoline to help ease inflation after the war in Iran sent prices of fossil fuels soaring.

item.title: As De Beers Bid Deadline Looms, Botswana Pushes for Control
item.description: Botswana is holding to its ambition of buying a controlling stake in De Beers from Anglo American Plc, potentially dampening interest from some private investors looking to acquire the diamond giant.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From the US CPI Report for March
item.description: Here are the key takeaways from the US consumer price index report for March, released Friday:

item.title: CoreWeave’s New Junk Bond Jumps Amid Meta, Anthropic Deals
item.description: CoreWeave Inc.’s $1.75 billion junk bond has rallied amid increased optimism about the cloud-infrastructure firm thanks to deals with some major technology companies.

item.title: US Government Pitches Gamers a Career as Air Traffic Controllers
item.description: Parents have long worried that video gaming corrupts their kids’ brains. Now the US government is proclaiming that all is not lost, and that in fact a lucrative career path awaits as an air-traffic controller.

item.title: What to Know About the EU’s New Digital Border Checks
item.description: The European Union’s new digital border control system has finally been fully implemented, changing how the majority of member states handle the arrival of hundreds of millions of people every year.

item.title: Bond Traders Cling to Bets on a Fed Rate Cut This Year After CPI
item.description: Bond traders trimmed wagers that the Federal Reserve will lower interest rates once this year after data confirmed that US inflation quickened in March as the Iran war led to higher gasoline prices.

item.title: Defense Stocks Tumble and Builders Rally on Ukraine Peace Hopes
item.description: European defense stocks slumped on Friday as a senior Ukraine official signaled progress toward a peace deal with Russia, while construction firms rose on bets that a ceasefire could trigger a post-war rebuilding boom.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Maintains Oil Exports From Key Red Sea Port for Now
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s oil exports via the Red Sea are holding steady for now, as the impact of a drone attack on its vital cross-country pipeline has yet to filter through.

item.title: US CPI Surges 0.9% in Largest Monthly Jump Since 2022 on Gas
item.description: US inflation surged in March by the most in nearly four years as the war with Iran sent gasoline prices skyrocketing.

item.title: Europe Weeks Away From Jet-Fuel Shortage, Airport Group Says
item.description: Europe faces a systemic jet-fuel shortage in three weeks’ time if the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted until then, a regional airport trade association warns.

item.title: Canada Job Gain of 14,100 Fails to Offset Recent Losses
item.description: The Canadian economy added a modest 14,100 jobs in March, in line with economists’ expectations but marking only a partial reversal of the 109,000 positions lost in the first two months of the year.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Surges Past Forecasts on Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Brazil’s inflation accelerated more than expected in March as the Iran war’s energy shock ripples through Latin America’s largest economy.

item.title: US Stocks’ Seven-Day Rally Stalls Ahead of CPI, US-Iran Talks
item.description: US equity futures stalled after a seven-day rally as investors weighed whether a fragile truce between Washington and Tehran can hold, and oil headed for its biggest weekly loss in nine months.

item.title: GQG’s Kersmanc Says Early Big Tech Exit Is Finally Paying Off
item.description: Brian Kersmanc at GQG Partners LLC says a decision to empty his funds of tech companies more than a year ago is finally starting to pay off.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CoreWeave, Docusign, Lumentum, Organon
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:50 a.m. in New York. Its seven-day winning streak, the longest run for the benchmark since October, looks set to be tested as traders prepare for CPI data.

item.title: Demand for Dollars in Currency Swaps Ebbs Alongside Global Risks
item.description: By at least one metric in the $9.5 trillion foreign-exchange markets, demand for the dollar is ebbing amid the tenuous ceasefire between the US and Iran.

item.title: Anthropic Will Rent CoreWeave AI Capacity to Power Claude
item.description: Anthropic PBC agreed to rent data center capacity from CoreWeave Inc. as part of efforts to handle increasing demand for its artificial intelligence services.

item.title: US CPI Data Offer First Look at Iran War’s Inflation Impact
item.description: Shoppers in the SoHo neighborhood of New York. Photographer: Michael Nagle/Bloomberg

item.title: Finance Titans Bet Mideast Resilience Will Outweigh War Fallout
item.description: Late last month, Blackstone Inc. announced the first inbound Gulf private equity deal since Iran started attacking Middle Eastern hubs, while Citigroup Inc.’s top boss fired off a 600-word memo underlining the bank’s enthusiasm for its business in the region.

item.title: Binance Offers Temporary Relocation to UAE-Based Employees
item.description: Binance Holdings Ltd. said it is offering temporary relocation to employees based in the United Arab Emirates, as the Iran conflict continues to impact countries across the Middle East.

item.title: Nuclear Power Takes Another Tiny Step Forward in the US
item.description: New Jersey’s governor removes a moratorium on new reactors.

item.title: Ares Plans Smaller Private Credit Fund After Record Predecessor
item.description: Ares Management Corp. is planning a significantly smaller flagship US direct lending fund than its previous record-breaking vehicle of $33.6 billion to speed up deployment of capital, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Urges Companies to Put AI Breakthroughs Before Profit
item.description: Leading booster for AI says companies are too focused on an immediate payoff
